data.body:
=== SESSION 3: bridge_state growth — reconciler-requirements investigation (VERIFIED) ===

CORRECTION of an earlier framing: prev_snapshot.json is NOT functionally redundant. Do NOT delete it.
Established by two independent adversarial subagents (red-team hunt-the-breaker + blue-team prove-removable)
that CONVERGED, plus direct code reads.

WHAT'S TRUE:
- prev_snapshot's DIRECTION-ARBITRATION role IS fully replaced by the per-binding baseline in bindings.json.
  PROVEN empirically, not just by reasoning: rollout ticket a118-fa8e recorded 30 consecutive zero-divergence
  shadow passes + a byte-identical flip pass; d6bd-95c9 hardcoded baseline-consumer-swap always-on; baseline-path
  direction tests pass (tests/.../diffing/test_baseline_arbitration.py::test_dryrun_arbitration_pinned_to_baseline
  feeds a STALE prev_snapshot and asserts the baseline wins). outbound_differ.py:795 -> outbound_fields.py:416-418
  overrides arbitration_prev = get_baseline(local_id); the prev_snapshot arg there is a fixture-only fallback = DEAD in prod.
- BUT arbitration is only 1 of ~6 consumers. prev_snapshot is STILL LOAD-BEARING as the EDGE-TRIGGER ANCESTOR:
  run_differs.py:198 calls differ.compute_mutations(prev_snapshot, curr_snapshot) UNCONDITIONALLY every pass;
  differ.py:299 "key in jira_state only -> inbound create". Empty prev => every Jira key looks new => re-emits the
  documented "2050+ inbound_create mutations on every pass" regression (reconcile.py:662-669). The baseline CANNOT
  cover this (keyed by local_id, confirmed-bindings only, no representation of UNBOUND native Jira issues).
- Other live prev_snapshot consumers with NO baseline equivalent: dual-identity invariant
  check_dual_identity_complete (run_differs.py:265, invariants.py); edge/level adoption coordination
  (binding_walk.py:205-212 skip_adopt_keys); corrupt-merge safety canary (_handle_corrupt_snapshot,
  reconcile.py:715-773 — also a tickets-branch-partial-merge tripwire); bridge_fsck drift oracle
  (bridge_fsck.py:257-291). NOTE: d6bd's stated keep-reason ("needed to seed baselines") is STALE —
  _advance_baselines reads curr not prev (reconcile.py:928) and seed_baselines_from_snapshot is never called in src/.

WHY IT'S STILL THE GROWTH DRIVER: prev_snapshot stores the FULL ACLI payload per issue (fetcher.py:413 + parent/
comment/issuelinks enrichments) — volatile fields (updated/created/watches/worklog) tick constantly across ~2050
issues => a new committed version nearly every pass (~4450 versions/155MB). Serialization is ALREADY canonical
(sort_keys+indent=2, dict-keyed) — reformatting buys nothing. It's advanced UNCONDITIONALLY every pass
(shutil.copy2 at reconcile.py:1008, no change-guard).

THE LEVER (verified-safe direction): prev_snapshot is OVER-PROVISIONED, not redundant. Its live consumers need at
most {key-set + rebar-id label + possibly the 5 mirrored fields}, NOT the full payload. So: NARROW the persisted
content to what the edge-trigger/dual-identity/fsck actually read (drops per-version size ~10-100x AND kills the
volatile-field churn that mints a version/pass) + CHANGE-GATE the advance. No delete, no history rewrite, no new ref,
no consumer broken. Open sizing question being verified now: is the legacy differ's key-in-both FIELD-DRIFT output
redundant with the outbound/classify path (=> prev shrinks to key->{rebar-id} map) or authoritative (=> retain 5 fields)?
Also a clean separate win: remove the DEAD outbound_differ prev_snapshot plumbing (OutboundDiffConfig.prev_snapshot
:364, :526, :795 threading, outbound_fields.py:416 fallback).

COORDINATION / SCOPE: bridge_state SIZE is UNOWNED. Active other session = epic 58b0 (comment/inbound-link SYNC
CORRECTNESS) — orthogonal to size, but shares the differ/field contract, so coordinate on any snapshot-content change.
Epic 6c9c-811f (dust-troth-naval, CLOSED) settled the storage posture: coordination state (pass/phase lock) moved to
refs/reconciler/*, but .bridge_state stays committed on the tickets branch with merge=ours as a derived cache
(WS-1 "re-home binding DATA off-branch" was EXPLICITLY DROPPED). So a separate-ref for bindings revives a dropped
decision; a separate self-resetting ref is viable ONLY for prev_snapshot (single ephemeral-CI writer, no non-reconciler
reader) but is dominated by simply narrowing it. bindings.json (219MB/987 versions) MUST stay on-branch — read by
ordinary clones via _ids.py:61 (Jira-key resolution) — its history bounding is a separate, lower-urgency problem.

DEFERRED (user parked pending this investigation): (1) how to keep a BOUNDED debug-history trail; (2) the existing
~337MB already sunk in history — accept + CI --filter=blob:none partial-clone (zero-risk) vs one-time reconciler-paused
all-clones-converged filter-repo reclaim (needs lockstep enforce_since re-pin rebar.toml:105 + flag-day force-push;
sync.py:90-92 --allow-unrelated-histories union DRAGS old blobs back from any un-converged peer, so periodic rewrite
is BROKEN, one-time only). Trust model survives a clean rewrite (ancestry-based, sigs cover event content-hash not
commit SHA — authorship.py) EXCEPT the enforce_since absolute SHA pin.
